title: 4 Establishing Policies for Originating AFT Transactions
body_text:
4	Establishing Policies for Originating AFT Transactions
4.1	Overview of Establishing Policies for Originating AFT Transactions
Financial institutions must comply with legal regulations that include but are not limited to identification requirements, record keeping requirements, and FINTRAC reporting requirements. Failure to do so can result in significant fines.
To ensure regulatory compliance and assist with risk mitigation, it is recommended that the financial institution establish policies on enrolling business members into AFT.
4.2	Policy on Reporting Suspicious AFT Transactions or Attempted AFT Transactions to FINTRAC
Financial institutions must establish a policy to submit a FINTRAC Suspicious Transaction or Attempted Transaction Report if there are reasonable grounds to suspect that an AFT Transaction of any amount is related to a money laundering or a terrorist financing offence. This includes a transaction conducted on behalf of an Account Holder, originated by an Account Holder, or received by an Account Holder even if the transaction is cancelled, declined, or abandoned after:
The Account Holder has entered negotiations or discussions to conduct the transaction
The financial institution has taken concrete measures to approve the transaction
See FINTRAC芒聙聶s Guideline 2 for examples of what constitutes a suspicious transaction.
4.3	Policy on Reporting Terrorist Property to FINTRAC
The financial institution must establish a policy on submitting a FINTRAC Terrorist Property Report if it has property in its possession or control that is owned or controlled by or on behalf of a terrorist or a terrorist group. This includes AFT Transactions conducted on behalf of Account Holders, originated by Account Holders, or received by Account Holders.
See FINTRAC芒聙聶s Guideline 3 for additional reporting requirements.
4.4	Policy on Payor芒聙聶s PAD Agreement
The Payor芒聙聶s PAD Agreement is an agreement made between the Originator (Payee) and the Payor. If the financial institution allows business member Originators to use a version of the Payor芒聙聶s PAD Agreement other than the Payor芒聙聶s PAD Agreement, Form 1696, the financial institution must review the form for CPA compliance. See CPA Rule H1, Pre-authorized Debits, for the mandatory and supplementary elements of a Payor芒聙聶s PAD Agreement.
Related Information
Payor芒聙聶s PAD Agreement,
Form 1696
4.5	Policy for Mitigating Risk and Liability
AFT files originated on behalf of or by a business member generate a certain amount of risk for the financial institution. The following are some examples of risk:
Insufficient funds in a business member芒聙聶s account can result in an overdraft when the Offsetting Entry for a Direct Deposit file is posted.
PADs originated by the business member can be returned by the Payor up to 90 days from the transaction date. The Payee芒聙聶s account can be overdrawn due to returned PADs.
Unauthorized access to AFT software can result in fraudulent AFT Transactions.
Providing an individual user with access to multiple Originator IDs for business member companies that are not associated to one another, introduces risk exposure. If the individual user芒聙聶s credentials are compromised, multiple business members are impacted.
The financial institution is responsible for losses associated with AFT Transactions regardless of whether the transactions are originated by a staff member or a business member. The following list provides numerous ways in which the financial institution can mitigate its risk and liability.
Know Your Member 芒聙聯 Due Diligence
Before setting up AFT service for a business member, confirm that the business member is credit worthy. Always follow the 芒聙聹know your member芒聙聺 rule. Consider the nature of the business member芒聙聶s business and its need for AFT services. Based on this information, determine the transaction type, the frequency of the service, if the debit should be fixed or variable, the maximum dollar limits for files and, where applicable, transactions.
Request a Line of Credit/Collateral
You can obtain a line of credit and/or collateral to cover the credit risk associated with this service. You can also ask for current financial statements and consent to do a Credit Reporting Agency investigation.
Enter into a Legal Agreement with the Business Member
Authorized financial institution and business member representatives must sign the appropriate service agreement
before
AFT service is initiated. This ensures that the business member芒聙聶s obligations and liabilities are legally acknowledged and accepted. Depending on the type of transaction that will be originated, complete the Letter of Undertaking for Pre-authorized Debit Plans, Form 1904, the Letter of Undertaking for Cash Management PADs, Form 2007, or the Direct Deposit Agreement, Form 2043.
Review Business Member芒聙聶s Payor芒聙聶s PAD Agreement
Prior to AFT set-up, review the business member芒聙聶s Payor芒聙聶s PAD Agreement, Form 1696, to ensure that it complies with CPA Rule H1, Pre-authorized Debits. The Payor芒聙聶s PAD Agreement is CPA compliant and can be used as a pro forma document. Review the agreement on an annual basis.
The business member is required to obtain a signed Payor芒聙聶s PAD Agreement, Form 1696, from each Payor. Consider auditing a random sample of these signed agreements to confirm that the business member is following the correct procedure.
Ensure Adequate Controls are in Place
Minimize risk by implementing an adequate set of controls including the following:
Limits
芒聙聯 Set reasonable file and transaction limits for each business member. Business members that originate AFT Transactions on
PaymentStream
AFT are subject to transaction and file limits. Determine the approximate value of the transactions and the approximate number of employees or Payors/customers to arrive at a maximum dollar value per file. This will help to identify potential exposure.
Note:	As a general guideline, Central聽1 will consider the financial institution芒聙聶s asset size when reviewing the maximum file limit that is listed on the AFT service request forms sent in by the financial institution. This controls the setup of an extremely high file limit. If necessary, Central聽1 will discuss file limit exceptions with the financial institution.
If a file limit is exceeded in
PaymentStream AFT
, financial institutions can manage AFT limits for financial institution and business member originators.
Confirmation Emails
芒聙聯 When a business member releases a file on
PaymentStream
AFT or uploads a file to
PaymentStream
AFT, a confirmation showing the number and value of transactions is emailed to the requested email addresses as set under Originator Information.
Frequency of Files
芒聙聯 Consider monitoring the frequency of a business member芒聙聶s AFT files. For example, if a business member indicates during set-up that it will send a monthly file but starts sending them bi-weekly, contact the business member to determine the reason. The frequency of AFT files should always correspond to the purpose for their origination.
Number of Chargebacks
芒聙聯 Monitor the number of items charged back to a business member芒聙聶s account. If the number of unauthorized PADs is out of the ordinary, determine if the business member is obtaining signed Payor芒聙聶s PAD Agreements from its customers.
Ensure Users are Set Up Securely
Although some business members upload files to
PaymentStream
AFT and some originate AFT Transactions on
PaymentStream
AFT, all require a unique user ID and password. To minimize risk, financial institutions are expected to communicate passwords and online access information to business members in a secure manner. It is recommended that the business member picks up a sealed envelope containing the information at the financial institution or that the financial institution personally delivers the envelope or sends it via courier.
Additionally, some business members may request that a user ID be provided to a third party to maintain and access the business member芒聙聶s AFT Originator ID on behalf of the business member. The third party user will be given a unique user ID to access only the business member芒聙聶s AFT Originator ID or, in some cases, multiple AFT Originator IDs that are associated with the same business member芒聙聶s companies. If the same third party individual has also been given authority by other companies to maintain and access their AFT Originator IDs on
PaymentStream
AFT, the third party user will be required to have a separate user ID for each company. The purpose of the separate user IDs is to isolate potential damage if the third party user芒聙聶s credentials become compromised.
Adequate AFT Staff Training
Provide adequate AFT training for financial institution staff. They should understand the need for due diligence, the need for credit approval and collateral, and the need for ongoing monitoring of activity and account balances. Depending upon the type of AFT service required, financial institution staff must be able to explain the terms and conditions of the Letter of Undertaking for Pre-authorized Debit Plans, Form 1904, the Letter of Undertaking for Cash Management PADs, Form 2007, or the Direct Deposit Agreement, Form 2043, to their business members.
Ensure that the front-line staff has access to the AFT procedures contained within the Payment Solutions Manual.
Related Information
Payor芒聙聶s PAD Agreement,
Form 1696
Letter of Undertaking for Pre-authorized Debit Plans,
Form 1904
Letter of Undertaking for Cash Management PADs,
Form 2007
Direct Deposit Agreement,
Form 2043
4.6	Policy on Internal Controls and AFT User Management
The financial institution is responsible for AFT security practices and internal controls. If the Originator is a business member of the financial institution, ensure that the Originator is trained on these practices and controls (as applicable).
Operations staff and external and internal auditors must establish internal controls for
PaymentStream
AFT access. It is recommended that procedures relating to the use of
PaymentStream
AFT be established to prevent or detect errors in a timely manner and to restrict access to
PaymentStream
AFT.
Note:	Financial institutions manage their user IDs, but Central聽1 will delete Originator IDs that have been inactive for 12 months or more.
Policies may include but are not limited to the following:
Ensuring that new users receive their one-time passwords, user IDs, and 2-Step Security tokens for
PaymentStream
AFT, in a secure and confidential manner
Note:	2-Step Security only applies to Business Members on
PaymentStream
AFT.
Training users not to share user IDs (each user ID is linked to the authorized Originator ID(s) and access level or assigned permissions)
Training users to use secure passwords that include at least eight characters, a combination of upper and lower case letters, numbers, and other non-alpha and non-numeric characters
Conducting management reviews and internal audits of
PaymentStream
AFT accessibility and activity
Limiting access to employees who require
PaymentStream
AFT to complete their job requirements
Establishing reasonable limits for all Originators
Limiting the use of multiple Client Centre user IDs for an individual
Specifying that each individual who requires access to an AFT Originator ID must request their own user ID and, if applicable 2-Step Security token. User IDs must
not
be shared.
END
